name: Dave Douglas
abstract: Dave Douglas (born March 24, 1963) is an American jazz trumpeter, composer, and educator. His career includes more than fifty recordings as a leader and more than 500 published compositions.

concerts.label: JVC Jazz Festival: Cassandra Wilson and Patricia Barber
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

concerts.label: Dave Douglas Jazz Workshop in Performance
In Your Ear Too: Dave Douglas and Keystone
Terry Riley's In C
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

about_quotes.speaker: Randy Sandke
about_quotes.evidence: I mean there are players like Dave Douglas, you know a very fine trumpet player who is really starting, just beginning to get the attention he deserves... I’ve heard some of his orchestral stuff. He’s a very talented guy.
about_quotes.doc_title: Randy Sandke interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 10, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Hentoff Nat
about_quotes.evidence: Meanwhile Stanley, a black writer, throws one brick at Dave Douglas, a white trumpet player, and not even that much of a brick. And he is right.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hentoff Nat — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Howard Mandel
about_quotes.evidence: up to Steve Lacy, Tim Berne, Dave Douglas, Myra Melford who I was hanging out with last night
about_quotes.doc_title: Howard Mandel interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 9, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Nat Hentoff
about_quotes.evidence: Dave Douglas is a man of great capacities but he has so many different combos.
about_quotes.doc_title: Nat Hentoff interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2007
